subject: PATIENT
heading: HOSPITAL COURSE:__page_no__2
text: hospital course: his is a 74 year old female with pmhx osa, htn and hyponatremia who presented to ed with difficulty speaking. patient admitted, mri brain shows multiple small punctate infarcts, ct brain no acute findings, cta carotids no evidence of critical stenosis, lipid panel reviewed with ldl 119, a1c 5.1, echocardiogram shows ef 55%. neurology consulted, started patient on aspirin, atorvastatin, plavix. physical therapy provided, suggested home care services, orders placed. patient provided with zio monitor patch on discharge. home hydrochlorothiazide discontinued in view of chronic worsening hyponatremia, continued home lisinopril, blood pressure satisfactory. patient to closely follow-up with pcp and neurology in 2 to 3 weeks, referrals provided. patient condition at discharge: stable

text: hospital course: his is a 74 year old female with pmhx osa, htn and hyponatremia who presented to ed with difficulty speaking. patient admitted, mri brain shows multiple small punctate infarcts, ct brain no acute findings, cta carotids no evidence of critical stenosis, lipid panel reviewed with ldl 119, a1c 5.1, echocardiogram shows ef 55%. neurology consulted, started patient on aspirin, atorvastatin, plavix. physical therapy provided, suggested home care services, orders placed. patient provided with zio monitor patch on discharge. home hydrochlorothiazide discontinued in view of chronic worsening hyponatremia, continued home lisinopril, blood pressure satisfactory. patient to closely follow-up with pcp and neurology in 2 to 3 weeks, referrals provided. patient condition at discharge: stable

subject: PATIENT
heading: CHIEF COMPLAINT__page_no__10
text: chief complaint difficulty speaking hpi: this is a 74 year old female with pmhx osa, htn and hyponatremia who presented to ed with difficulty speaking. patient states that she had a diffuse headache earlier this morning, so she took an aspirin. she had a phone call with her husband who is driving to [address] for his work, and he was concerned about her difficulty speaking and thought she was having a stroke. while patient was driving, she reportedly had a low impact car accident as she went off the road and hit a mailbox. ems was called. glucose level was 80's in the field, so she was given some oral glucose and brought to er. in er, she was afebrile with bp 222/100. initial lab work was significant for na 133 and hgb 15.6. cta head/neck and ct head were unrevealing. she was treated with full dose aspirin. case was discussed with the stroke neurologist, and she was admitted for further work up.

text: chief complaint difficulty speaking hpi: this is a 74 year old female with pmhx osa, htn and hyponatremia who presented to ed with difficulty speaking. patient states that she had a diffuse headache earlier this morning, so she took an aspirin. she had a phone call with her husband who is driving to [address] for his work, and he was concerned about her difficulty speaking and thought she was having a stroke. while patient was driving, she reportedly had a low impact car accident as she went off the road and hit a mailbox. ems was called. glucose level was 80's in the field, so she was given some oral glucose and brought to er. in er, she was afebrile with bp 222/100. initial lab work was significant for na 133 and hgb 15.6. cta head/neck and ct head were unrevealing. she was treated with full dose aspirin. case was discussed with the stroke neurologist, and she was admitted for further work up.

subject: PATIENT
heading: Reason for Appointment :: Interim History:__page_no__15
text: reason for appointment interim history: transition of care inpatient name of the hospital [facility_name] transition from inpatient hospitalization transition to home (own) admission date 08/27/2024 30 day readmission no number of in patient admissions in last 12 months. 1 discharge date 08/29/2024 discharge summary report received yes per ccf records on 08/29/2024: hospital course: his is a 74 year old female with pmhx osa, htn and hyponatremia who presented to ed with difficultyspeaking. patient admitted, mri brain shows multiple small punctate infarcts, ct brain no acute findings, cta carotids no evidence of critical stenosis, lipid panel reviewed with ldl 119, a1c 5.1, echocardiogram shows ef 55%. neurology consulted, started patient on aspirin, atorvastatin, plavix. physical therapy provided, suggested home care services, orders placed. patient provided with zio monitor patch on discharge. home hydrochlorothiazide discontinued in view of chronic worsening hyponatremia, continued home lisinopril, blood pressure satisfactory. patient to closely follow-up with pcp and neurology in 2 to 3 weeks, referrals provided. patient condition at discharge: stable date discharge summary was received: 08/30/2024 final discharge diagnoses received yes diagnosis: acute multiple small punctate infarcts hypertensive urgency expressive aphasia, improved chronic hyponatremia post-discharge other consultation/followup yes neurologist-[provider_name] when: in 3 weeks results pending post-discharge none discharged with home care or ancillary therapy: yes [facility_name] does the patient have a care manager? no but cm t sloas was notified on 08/30/2024 due to high risk diagnosis assistance needed with adl's: no oral nutritional supplements given inpatient or prescribed homegoing no initial contact date 08/30/2024 initial contact within 2 business days of discharge yes initial contact person patient
